name: Breadcrumbs
description: Breadcrumbs show where the operator is within the portal's hierarchy and let them step back up it — from a single vehicle, to the vehicle list, to the fleet. They orient users in deep desktop navigation.
keyRule: Use breadcrumbs when content sits more than one level deep. They complement — not replace — the left navigation drawer.

variants.name: Standard
variants.whenToUse: Show the full path for shallow hierarchies.

variants.name: Collapsed
variants.whenToUse: Hide middle levels behind "…" for deep paths.

variants.name: With menu crumb
variants.whenToUse: A crumb opens a menu of sibling pages.

anatomy.name: Current page
anatomy.description: the last crumb, not a link.

anatomy.name: Overflow
anatomy.description: optional collapse ("…") for very deep paths.

behaviors.name: Navigation
behaviors.description: Each crumb except the last navigates to that ancestor; the current page is plain text.

behaviors.name: Truncation
behaviors.description: Long labels truncate with a tooltip; deep paths collapse the middle.

props.name: items
props.description: Full ancestor path, ending with the current page.

props.name: separator
props.description: Separator glyph between crumbs (decorative, hidden from assistive tech).

props.name: maxVisible
props.description: Collapse middle levels behind "…" for deep paths.

doThis: Make the current page the non-link last crumb.
dontDoThis: Don't use breadcrumbs as the primary navigation.
Don't show a one-level trail (no value).
Don't make the current page a link to itself.
accessibility: Wrap in a nav labelled "Breadcrumb" with an ordered list.
Separators are decorative (hidden from assistive tech).
Links are keyboard reachable with visible focus.

related.name: Tabs
related.why: For switching sibling views at the same level

related.name: PageHeader
related.why: Repurposed as the back-link on PageHeader's detail-page variant
